applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused post-conv kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Fused add, min, GELU, and multiply operations into a single Triton kernel. Used manual tanh implementation for GELU (tl.libdevice.tanh unavailable on ROCm). BLOCK_SIZE=256 for AMD wavefront alignment (multiple of 64).

top_bottlenecks: Triton kernel launch overhead may be limiting factor

notes: Initial baseline established: 2.57ms vs 3.86ms reference (1.50x speedup, score 65.0)
Key fix from failed attempt: replaced tl.libdevice.tanh with manual implementation using tl.math.exp
Manual tanh: (exp(2x) - 1) / (exp(2x) + 1) with clamping to [-10, 10] to prevent overflow

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output at all — the agent crashed or hung again. This is the 3rd consecutive silent failure. The agent needs extremely prescriptive, step-by-step instructions with explicit shell c

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output — 4th consecutive silent crash. The working baseline at /workspace/generated_kernel.py achieves score 65. Need extremely prescriptive atomic instructions to prevent crashes
